reasoning: The FRED time series for Cleveland Fed 1-Year Expected Inflation fluctuates quite a bit without a very strong long-term persistent trend, with recent values ranging between 2.38 and 2.88. The most recent value is 2.704. Although 2.704 is slightly above the 12-month median (which would normally suggest a naive expectation <0.5 for an increase due to mean reversion), the FRED linear trend model predicts a slight upward trajectory projecting to 2.77 by December 2025, assigning a probability of 0.58 that it will exceed 2.704. Balancing the linear trend (suggesting a slight tilt towards an increase) and the natural mean-reverting property of this series (pushing the estimate back toward the median of 2.69), I set the near-term probability slightly above 0.5. Following the rule for increased uncertainty over a longer horizon, I move the probability for the farther resolution date closer to 0.5.
